steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that a kestrel's surroundings significantly affect its hormone levels, then uses 'Therefore' to signal an expected finding. The blank must report a difference in hormone levels between wild and captive kestrels.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "hormone levels were higher in wild males than in captive male canaries.": The passage is about wild vs. captive kestrels, not across species. ✗
  • "wild males and captive males usually exhibited very similar hormone levels.": 'Therefore' signals that different surroundings should produce different levels — similarity contradicts the logic. ✗
  • "significant differences in hormone levels between individuals were found for both wild males and captive males.": Individual variation is unrelated to the environmental effect the passage describes. ✗
  • "levels of androgen hormones during breeding season the higher in wild males than they are in captive males.": This reports a difference in hormone levels tied directly to setting (wild vs. captive), completing the 'Therefore' inference. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
